rationale.R1.1: The 'twofold contribution' framing conflates benchmark curation with comparative evaluation; the gap statement (saturation argument) and the contribution claim (RigorDTI as remedy) need sharper logical linkage and separation.
rationale.R1.2: A direct logical contradiction is present: Sheridan et al. 2024 is credited with 'first introducing' the time-split protocol, yet the very next sentence asserts time-based splitting 'has been standard cheminformatics practice for over a decade.' These cannot both be accurate simultaneously.
rationale.R1.3: The stated ranges yield a dispersion increase of ~23× (random spread 0.02 RMSE vs. time-split spread 0.47 RMSE), not the '6× increase' claimed in the text. This internal numerical inconsistency is a load-bearing error for a paper whose core argument rests on that dispersion contrast.
rationale.R1.6: Load-bearing for 'improve this draft.' The prose contains multiple overclaiming phrases ('decisively resolves the long-standing question,' 'transformative advance,' 'substantial leap forward,' 'definitive new standard') that undermine credibility and require tonal recalibration consistent with hedged scientific writing.
rationale.R1.7: The Sheridan et al. 2024 citation is used to anchor a 'first introduction' claim that contradicts the immediately following assertion of decade-long standard practice; the attribution needs either correction or reconciliation to avoid misrepresenting prior work.
